articles.title: Banda sizzles at 45.6℃ as heatwave grips Uttar Pradesh - The Hindu
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Uttar Pradesh
articles.full_text:
Banda in Uttar Pradesh's Bundelkhand region recorded the highest temperature in the State at a sweltering 45.6 degrees Celsius on Tuesday (April 28, 2026) as heatwave conditions prevailed across several regions, according to the India Meteorological Department (IMD).
Among other hot spots, Orai recorded 43.8 degrees Celsius, followed by Jhansi at 42.4 degrees Celsius, while Fatehgarh and Hamirpur both registered 42.2 degrees Celsius. Agra too witnessed extreme weather at 42.1 degrees Celsius, reflecting the widespread intensity of the heatwave across the State.
Published - April 29, 2026 04:25 pm IST

articles.title: Weather Today: आगरा में मौसम का बदलाव, बूंदाबांदी ने दी गर्मी से राहत; आज और कल बारिश का अनुमान - Jagran
articles.search_term: लू (Agra OR Aligarh OR Allahabad OR "Ambedkar Nagar" OR "Amethi (Chatrapati Sahuji Mahraj Nagar)" OR "Amroha (J.P. Nagar)" OR Auraiya OR Azamgarh OR Baghpat OR Bahraich OR Ballia OR Balrampur OR Banda OR Barabanki OR Bareilly OR Basti OR Bhadohi OR Bijnor OR Budaun OR Bulandshahr OR Chandauli OR Chitrakoot OR Deoria OR Etah OR Etawah OR Faizabad OR Farrukhabad OR Fatehpur OR Firozabad OR "Gautam Buddha Nagar" OR Ghaziabad OR Ghazipur OR Gonda OR Gorakhpur OR Hamirpur OR "Hapur (Panchsheel Nagar)" OR Hardoi OR Hathras OR Jalaun OR Jaunpur OR Jhansi OR Kannauj OR "Kanpur Dehat" OR "Kanpur Nagar" OR "Kanshiram Nagar (Kasganj)" OR Kaushambi OR "Kushinagar (Padrauna)" OR "Lakhimpur - Kheri" OR Lalitpur OR Lucknow OR Maharajganj OR Mahoba OR Mainpuri OR Mathura OR Mau OR Meerut OR Mirzapur OR Moradabad OR Muzaffarnagar OR Pilibhit OR Pratapgarh OR RaeBareli OR Rampur OR Saharanpur OR "Sambhal (Bhim Nagar)" OR "Sant Kabir Nagar" OR Shahjahanpur OR "Shamali (Prabuddh Nagar)" OR Shravasti OR "Siddharth Nagar" OR Sitapur OR Sonbhadra OR Sultanpur OR Unnao OR Varanasi) Uttar Pradesh
articles.full_text:
Weather Today: आगरा में मौसम का बदलाव, बूंदाबांदी ने दी गर्मी से राहत; आज और कल बारिश का अनुमान
मौसम विभाग ने बुधवार व गुरुवार को बादल छाए रहने और गरज के साथ बौछारें पड़ने का अनुमान जताया है, जिससे तापमान में और गिरावट आएगी। ...और पढ़ें
समय कम है?
जानिए मुख्य बातें और खबर का सार एक नजर में
जागरण संवाददाता, आगरा। शहर में पश्चिमी विक्षोभ की सक्रियता से मंगलवार को मौसम में बदलाव देखने को मिला। सोमवार देर रात शहर में तेज हवा के साथ बूंदाबांदी जरूर हुई, लेकिन रात का तापमान बढ़ गया। रात करवटें बदलते हुए बीती। मंगलवार को हल्के बादलों की वजह से अधिकतम तापमान में गिरावट आने से लू से भी राहत मिली।
मौसम विज्ञान विभाग ने बुधवार व गुरुवार को बादल छाए रहने व गरज के साथ बौछार पड़ने का पूर्वानुमान जताया है। तापमान में भी गिरावट देखने को मिलेगी। आगरा में बुधवार को सुबह बादलों की आवाजाही है और तेज हवा चल रही है। हालांकि अभी बौछार पड़ने का इंतजार है।
सोमवार देर रात शहर में तेज हवा के साथ हुई बूंदाबांदी
आगरा में शनिवार को अधिकतम तापमान 44.5 डिग्री सेल्सियस तक पहुंच गया था। दिन का तापमान बढ़ने के साथ ही रात के तापमान में भी वृद्धि से लोग परेशान हो उठे थे। सोमवार रात 11 बजे के बाद मौसम में बदलाव आया था। तेज हवा चलने के साथ दो बार बूंदाबांदी हुई थी। इससे मंगलवार को तापमान में गिरावट आने और लू चलना बंद होने से गर्मी से परेशान लोगों को राहत मिली।
मंगलवार को अधिकतम तापमान में गिरावट, लू से राहत
अधिकतम तापमान 42.1 डिग्री सेल्सियस और न्यूनतम तापमान 27.8 डिग्री सेल्सियस रहा। सामान्य से अधिकतम तापमान दो डिग्री सेल्सियस और न्यूनतम तापमान चार डिग्री सेल्सियस अधिक रहा। सोमवार को अधिकतम तापमान 44.4 डिग्री सेल्सियस और न्यूनतम तापमान 27.5 डिग्री सेल्सियस रहा था।
खबरें और भी
आज और कल के मौसम का अनुमान
मौसम विज्ञानी मो. दानिश ने बताया कि बुधवार व गुरुवार को बादल छाए रहने के साथ गरज के साथ बौछार पड़ सकती हैं। दिन व रात के तापमान में गिरावट भी देखने को मिलेगी।

articles.title: Agra: Heatwave in North India triggers decline in tourist footfall at Taj Mahal - Dailyhunt
articles.search_term: heatwave (Agra OR Aligarh OR Allahabad OR "Ambedkar Nagar" OR "Amethi (Chatrapati Sahuji Mahraj Nagar)" OR "Amroha (J.P. Nagar)" OR Auraiya OR Azamgarh OR Baghpat OR Bahraich OR Ballia OR Balrampur OR Banda OR Barabanki OR Bareilly OR Basti OR Bhadohi OR Bijnor OR Budaun OR Bulandshahr OR Chandauli OR Chitrakoot OR Deoria OR Etah OR Etawah OR Faizabad OR Farrukhabad OR Fatehpur OR Firozabad OR "Gautam Buddha Nagar" OR Ghaziabad OR Ghazipur OR Gonda OR Gorakhpur OR Hamirpur OR "Hapur (Panchsheel Nagar)" OR Hardoi OR Hathras OR Jalaun OR Jaunpur OR Jhansi OR Kannauj OR "Kanpur Dehat" OR "Kanpur Nagar" OR "Kanshiram Nagar (Kasganj)" OR Kaushambi OR "Kushinagar (Padrauna)" OR "Lakhimpur - Kheri" OR Lalitpur OR Lucknow OR Maharajganj OR Mahoba OR Mainpuri OR Mathura OR Mau OR Meerut OR Mirzapur OR Moradabad OR Muzaffarnagar OR Pilibhit OR Pratapgarh OR RaeBareli OR Rampur OR Saharanpur OR "Sambhal (Bhim Nagar)" OR "Sant Kabir Nagar" OR Shahjahanpur OR "Shamali (Prabuddh Nagar)" OR Shravasti OR "Siddharth Nagar" OR Sitapur OR Sonbhadra OR Sultanpur OR Unnao OR Varanasi) Uttar Pradesh
articles.full_text:
Agra (Uttar Pradesh) [India], April 29 (ANI): A severe heatwave across Northern India on Tuesday resulted in a noticeable drop in the number of tourists visiting the Taj Mahal in Agra.
With temperatures rising sharply, the usually bustling monument saw thinned crowds as visitors struggled to cope with the extreme weather conditions.
According to the officials, heatwaves in North India resulted in a decrease in the footfall of tourists at the Taj Mahal.
Speaking to ANI, a tourist, Anjali Singh, said, "Children wanted to visit the Taj Mahal. It is very hot here… We have completed our tour within an hour… We had to face a lot of problems due to the heat…"
On Sunday, the India Meteorological Department said that maximum temperatures are in the range of 40-46 degrees Celsius over most parts of the country, except the western Himalayan region, northeast Bihar and northeast India, where temperatures remain below 36 degrees Celsius.
The weather agency further informed that the highest maximum temperature of 46.9 degrees Celsius was recorded at Akola.
The India Meteorological Department forecasted heat wave to severe heat wave conditions in isolated pockets of Himachal Pradesh on April 26 and heat wave conditions on April 27.
The weather department said heat wave conditions are very likely in isolated pockets over Jammu and Kashmir on April 26 and 27, and across Punjab, Haryana, Chandigarh, Uttar Pradesh, Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, Bihar and Gujarat during the same period, with similar conditions expected over parts of Chhattisgarh till April 28.
Earlier on Sunday, the district administration in Prayagraj issued an advisory over rising temperatures, and as residents are struggling amid intense heat and harsh sunlight, officials said.
"We have changed the school timings, and schools will be closed by 12 pm so that children do not have to stay in the sun unnecessarily. Whenever anyone goes outside at this time, they should cover their head. The body should remain hydrated, and one should drink adequate amounts of water regularly to avoid dehydration," District Magistrate Manish Kumar Verma told ANI.
Urging people to avoid outings in the heat, he said, "And if they do, they should take full precautions. We are installing water ATMs wherever needed."
On Sunday, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) issued a heat wave alert for parts of northwest and central India, warning that scorching conditions are likely to continue over the next three days.
The weather department said heat wave conditions are very likely in isolated/some parts of isolated pockets of Jammu-Kashmir, Punjab, Haryana, Chandigarh & Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, Rajasthan, West Madhya Pradesh, Vidarbha, Bihar, Gujarat on June 27. A heat wave also gripped Varanasi as people struggled for relief.
Avoid prolonged exposure to heat. Wear lightweight, light-colored, and loose cotton clothing. Cover your head using a cloth, hat, or umbrella when outdoors, IMD said. (ANI)
